_meta.description: Oceania subset of the retirement city dataset, structured to match the master schema (the Lisbon entry as template). All scores are 1-10 (10 = best). Monthly costs in USD for a single person, comfortable lifestyle.
_meta.modeled_on: Lisbon (id 2 in master dataset): a safe, coastal, developed city with strong healthcare, good internet, an established expat community, and retiree-friendly visa access. Australian and New Zealand coastal cities are the closest Oceania analogues.

schema_notes.scores: All *_score fields are 1-10 integers. 10 = best/most favorable for retirees.
schema_notes.monthly_cost: USD, single person, comfortable lifestyle (not budget, not luxury).
schema_notes.visa_ease_score: 10 = easy long-stay visa or retirement visa available. 1 = very restrictive. NOTE: Australia has no dedicated retirement visa (old subclasses 405/410 are closed), so its cities score low here despite high livability. New Zealand's Temporary Retirement Category requires a large investment, so it scores moderate.
schema_notes.healthcare_score: 10 = world-class private hospitals, low cost. 1 = poor infrastructure.
schema_notes.safety_score: 10 = very safe. 1 = high crime or instability.
schema_notes.climate_score: 10 = year-round pleasant. Subjective; filter by climate_type.
schema_notes.overall_retirement_score: Weighted composite. Recalculate when user adjusts priority weights.
schema_notes.ids: Integer ids here are local to this Oceania file (1-42). The master table uses UUID primary keys, so renumber or drop on import as needed.

_meta.supabase_table_sql: CREATE TABLE retirement_cities (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), city TEXT NOT NULL, country TEXT NOT NULL, region TEXT NOT NULL, subregion TEXT, latitude FLOAT, longitude FLOAT, monthly_cost_usd INTEGER, lifestyle_label TEXT, description TEXT, tags TEXT[], visa_ease_score INTEGER, healthcare_score INTEGER, safety_score INTEGER, climate_score INTEGER, expat_community_score INTEGER, internet_score INTEGER, overall_retirement_score FLOAT, climate_type TEXT, language TEXT, currency TEXT, time_zone TEXT, popular_neighborhoods TEXT[], visa_notes TEXT, healthcare_notes TEXT, best_for TEXT[], avoid_if TEXT[], expense_shares JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW());

cities.description: Australia's harbour city — iconic beaches, world-class healthcare, and an outdoor lifestyle, at a premium price.
cities.visa_notes: No dedicated retirement visa. Retirees typically come via Parent (subclass 143/103), Investor, or extended visitor routes. Pathways are slow and costly.
cities.healthcare_notes: Universal Medicare for residents plus excellent private hospitals. Among the best healthcare in the world; access depends on residency status.
best_for: Those wanting top-tier healthcare and safety
avoid_if: Anyone needing an easy long-stay visa

cities.description: Australia's cultural capital — celebrated coffee, the arts, and laneway dining, with four distinct seasons.
cities.visa_notes: No retirement visa. Long stays via Parent, Investor, or visitor visas only. Residency is achievable but slow and expensive.
cities.healthcare_notes: Medicare for residents plus leading private hospitals (Epworth, Cabrini). World-class standards.

cities.description: Sunny subtropical capital of Queensland — relaxed riverside living, warm winters, and a growing expat draw.
cities.visa_notes: No retirement visa. Parent, Investor, or visitor pathways apply. Queensland's lower costs make it popular among Australian retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Medicare plus strong private hospitals (Mater, St Andrew's). Excellent quality.

cities.description: Sun-drenched Indian Ocean city — pristine beaches, a Mediterranean climate, and one of the world's most isolated capitals.
cities.visa_notes: No retirement visa. Parent, Investor, or visitor routes. Same national rules as the rest of Australia.
cities.healthcare_notes: Medicare plus quality private hospitals (St John of God, Hollywood). Strong standards; remoteness can mean travel for niche specialists.
best_for: Those who value quiet and space
avoid_if: Those wanting cultural buzz of bigger cities

cities.description: Elegant, affordable 'City of Churches' — wine country on the doorstep, easy pace, and a Mediterranean climate.
cities.visa_notes: No retirement visa. Standard Parent, Investor, or visitor routes apply.
cities.healthcare_notes: Medicare plus solid private hospitals. Excellent quality, shorter waits than larger capitals.

cities.description: Australia's beach-resort strip — 57 km of surf coast, golf, and a large retiree and snowbird population.
cities.visa_notes: No retirement visa. Parent, Investor, or visitor pathways. Popular with domestic retirees relocating from cooler states.
cities.healthcare_notes: Medicare plus Gold Coast University Hospital and private options. Good access, large retiree-oriented services.
best_for: Those wanting an active social scene

cities.description: Laid-back Queensland coastline of beach towns and hinterland — quieter than the Gold Coast, with the same warm climate.
cities.visa_notes: No retirement visa. Parent, Investor, or visitor routes apply nationally.
cities.healthcare_notes: Medicare plus Sunshine Coast University Hospital and private clinics. Strong regional healthcare.
best_for: Those wanting a slower coastal pace

cities.description: Tropical gateway to the Great Barrier Reef and Daintree — warm year-round, relaxed, and outdoorsy.
cities.visa_notes: No retirement visa. Parent, Investor, or visitor routes apply.
cities.healthcare_notes: Cairns Hospital plus private clinics. Good for a regional centre; complex cases may travel to Brisbane.
avoid_if: Those who dislike heat and humidity

cities.description: Australia's tropical 'Top End' capital — multicultural, frontier-flavoured, with a wet/dry tropical climate.
cities.visa_notes: No retirement visa. Parent, Investor, or visitor routes apply nationally.
cities.healthcare_notes: Royal Darwin Hospital plus limited private options. Adequate; specialist care often means travel south.
avoid_if: Anyone sensitive to intense heat and wet-season storms

cities.description: Tasmania's charming harbour capital — historic, artsy (MONA), and surrounded by wilderness, with a cool maritime climate.
cities.visa_notes: No retirement visa. Parent, Investor, or visitor routes apply.
cities.healthcare_notes: Royal Hobart Hospital plus private options. Good quality; smaller specialist pool than mainland capitals.
best_for: Those wanting an affordable, walkable capital
avoid_if: Those needing a wide range of specialists locally

cities.description: Australia's planned capital — green, spacious, and high-income, with museums, galleries, and four seasons.
cities.visa_notes: No retirement visa. Parent, Investor, or visitor routes apply.
cities.healthcare_notes: Canberra Hospital plus Calvary private. Excellent standards; close to Sydney for niche specialists.
avoid_if: Those wanting low costs or nightlife

cities.description: Coastal university city south of Sydney — surf beaches backed by an escarpment, calmer and cheaper than the capital.
cities.visa_notes: No retirement visa. Parent, Investor, or visitor routes apply nationally.
cities.healthcare_notes: Wollongong Hospital plus private clinics; Sydney's major hospitals are ~1.5 hours away.
avoid_if: Those needing a major hospital next door

cities.description: Revitalised harbour and surf city north of Sydney — beaches, a relaxed vibe, and more affordable coastal living.
cities.visa_notes: No retirement visa. Parent, Investor, or visitor routes apply.
cities.healthcare_notes: John Hunter Hospital is a major regional centre, plus private options. Strong healthcare for its size.
best_for: Those wanting Sydney-lite at lower cost

cities.description: Waterfront city near Melbourne and the Great Ocean Road — affordable, walkable, and steadily gentrifying.
cities.visa_notes: No retirement visa. Parent, Investor, or visitor routes apply.
cities.healthcare_notes: University Hospital Geelong plus private options; Melbourne's major hospitals ~1 hour away.
avoid_if: Those wanting a lively urban core

cities.description: Dry-tropics city in North Queensland — sunny, affordable, and a gateway to Magnetic Island and the reef.
cities.visa_notes: No retirement visa. Parent, Investor, or visitor routes apply.
cities.healthcare_notes: Townsville University Hospital is a major northern referral centre, plus Mater private. Solid regional care.
avoid_if: Those sensitive to heat and cyclone season

cities.description: New Zealand's largest city — the 'City of Sails' between two harbours, multicultural and outdoorsy, with mild weather.
cities.visa_notes: Temporary Retirement Category visa for 66+ requires ~NZD 750,000 investment plus NZD 500,000 living funds and income. Renewable, not a residency path.
cities.healthcare_notes: Strong public system plus private hospitals (Mercy, Southern Cross). High quality; public waits can be long.
best_for: Those wanting a mild, green city
avoid_if: Those who can't meet the investment visa threshold

cities.description: NZ's compact, walkable capital — celebrated coffee and arts scene wrapped around a dramatic harbour (and known for wind).
cities.visa_notes: Same national Temporary Retirement Category rules: substantial investment and funds required for 66+.
cities.healthcare_notes: Wellington Regional Hospital plus private options. High quality public and private care.
avoid_if: Those put off by the famous gales

cities.description: The rebuilt 'Garden City' of the South Island — spacious, flat, and affordable, near beaches and the Southern Alps.
cities.visa_notes: National Temporary Retirement Category rules apply (investment and funds for 66+).
cities.healthcare_notes: Christchurch Hospital is a major South Island centre, plus private options. Strong care.
best_for: Those wanting space at lower cost

cities.description: Alpine lakeside resort town — stunning scenery, year-round adventure, and a premium price tag.
cities.visa_notes: National Temporary Retirement Category rules apply (substantial investment and funds for 66+).
cities.healthcare_notes: Lakes District Hospital handles basics; serious cases transfer to Dunedin or Invercargill.
avoid_if: Those needing a major hospital locally

cities.description: Historic, Scottish-flavoured university city on the South Island coast — affordable, walkable, and wildlife-rich.
cities.visa_notes: National Temporary Retirement Category rules apply.
cities.healthcare_notes: Dunedin Hospital is a teaching hospital and regional referral centre. Good care for its size.

cities.description: Riverside inland city in the Waikato — affordable, green, and central, with famous gardens and easy Auckland access.
cities.visa_notes: National Temporary Retirement Category rules apply.
cities.healthcare_notes: Waikato Hospital is a major regional centre, plus private options. Strong care.

cities.description: Sunny Bay of Plenty harbour city with Mount Maunganui's beaches — one of NZ's most popular retirement destinations.
cities.visa_notes: National Temporary Retirement Category rules apply.
cities.healthcare_notes: Tauranga Hospital plus private options. Good regional healthcare; popular with retirees so services are oriented to them.
best_for: Those wanting an established retiree community

cities.description: Sunniest city in NZ — an artsy coastal town at the top of the South Island, gateway to Abel Tasman National Park.
cities.visa_notes: National Temporary Retirement Category rules apply.
cities.healthcare_notes: Nelson Hospital plus private clinics. Good regional care; complex cases may travel to Christchurch.
best_for: Those wanting a calm coastal town

cities.description: Art Deco seaside city in Hawke's Bay wine country — sunny, walkable, and famously photogenic.
cities.visa_notes: National Temporary Retirement Category rules apply.
cities.healthcare_notes: Hawke's Bay Hospital (Hastings) serves the region, plus local clinics. Solid regional care.

cities.description: Geothermal lakeside town and Maori cultural heartland — affordable, scenic, with a distinctive sulphur-tinged setting.
cities.visa_notes: National Temporary Retirement Category rules apply.
cities.healthcare_notes: Rotorua Hospital handles general care; complex cases transfer to Waikato or Tauranga.
avoid_if: Those bothered by the sulphur smell

cities.description: Quieter alpine-lake alternative to Queenstown — stunning, active, and increasingly upscale.
cities.visa_notes: National Temporary Retirement Category rules apply.
cities.healthcare_notes: Small Wanaka clinic; hospital care is in Dunedin or Invercargill. Limited local healthcare.
best_for: Scenery seekers wanting calm over Queenstown's bustle

cities.description: Sunny heart of the Marlborough wine region — affordable, quiet, and surrounded by vineyards.
cities.visa_notes: National Temporary Retirement Category rules apply.
cities.healthcare_notes: Wairau Hospital handles general care; Nelson and Christchurch for complex cases.

cities.description: Flat, affordable university city in the Manawatu — practical, central, and budget-friendly.
cities.visa_notes: National Temporary Retirement Category rules apply.
cities.healthcare_notes: Palmerston North Hospital is a regional referral centre. Solid care.

cities.description: NZ's southernmost city — the country's most affordable, with wide streets, friendly locals, and a cool climate.
cities.visa_notes: National Temporary Retirement Category rules apply.
cities.healthcare_notes: Southland Hospital handles general care; Dunedin for complex cases.
best_for: Those wanting quiet and friendly community

cities.description: Fiji's lively, multicultural capital — affordable tropical living and the Pacific's main regional hub (though rainy).
cities.visa_notes: No formal retirement visa, but long-stay/residence permits are obtainable with proof of income or investment. English widely spoken eases the process.
cities.healthcare_notes: Public hospitals are basic; expats use private clinics. Serious cases often fly to Australia or New Zealand.

cities.description: Fiji's sunny, tourist-friendly west coast gateway — drier than Suva, near the Mamanuca and Yasawa islands.
cities.visa_notes: Same national Fiji long-stay/residence permit routes (proof of income or investment).
cities.healthcare_notes: Local hospital is basic; private clinics in Nadi/Denarau. Complex cases fly to Australia or NZ.
best_for: Those wanting an international airport nearby

cities.description: Fiji's 'Sugar City' on the western coast — quieter and cheaper than Nadi, with an authentic local feel.
cities.visa_notes: Same national Fiji long-stay/residence permit routes.
cities.healthcare_notes: Lautoka Hospital is a regional facility but under-resourced; serious cases fly abroad.
best_for: Those wanting an authentic local pace

cities.description: Relaxed harbour capital of Vanuatu — tax-friendly, tropical, and one of the easier Pacific nations for long-stay residency.
cities.visa_notes: Long-stay residence permits and a residency-by-investment program are available. No personal income tax is a notable draw.
cities.healthcare_notes: Vila Central Hospital plus private clinics; limited capacity. Serious cases evacuate to Australia or NZ.
best_for: Those wanting an easier Pacific residency

cities.description: Samoa's easygoing capital — traditional Polynesian culture, warm seas, and a slow island pace.
cities.visa_notes: No dedicated retirement visa; long stays via extended permits. Process is informal and case-by-case.
cities.healthcare_notes: Tupua Tamasese Meaole Hospital handles basics; serious cases travel to New Zealand.
best_for: Those wanting a slow Polynesian pace

cities.description: Capital of the Kingdom of Tonga — quiet, deeply traditional, and among the most affordable Pacific options.
cities.visa_notes: No retirement visa; long stays via renewable permits, handled case-by-case.
cities.healthcare_notes: Vaiola Hospital handles basics; serious cases evacuate to New Zealand or Australia.

cities.description: Lush volcanic main island of the Cook Islands — turquoise lagoon, friendly community, and NZ-linked stability.
cities.visa_notes: No automatic residency; long stays via permits. The Cook Islands are self-governing in free association with New Zealand, so rules differ from NZ proper.
cities.healthcare_notes: Rarotonga Hospital handles basics; complex cases fly to New Zealand. Very limited specialist care.
best_for: Those wanting a safe, friendly island

cities.description: Capital of Tahiti and gateway to Bora Bora — French infrastructure and culture in a stunning, but pricey, island setting.
cities.visa_notes: As a French overseas collectivity, French/EU long-stay visa rules apply. A French long-stay visa (visa de long sejour) is the standard route for retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Centre Hospitalier de Polynesie Francaise offers French-standard care — the best in the region — though costs are high.
best_for: Those wanting strong healthcare in the tropics

cities.description: Cosmopolitan French capital of New Caledonia — Riviera-style bays, the world's largest lagoon, and European amenities.
cities.visa_notes: French overseas territory — French/EU long-stay visa rules apply. French long-stay visa is the route for retirees. Note: periodic political tensions over independence.
cities.healthcare_notes: Centre Hospitalier Territorial provides French-standard care, the best in the immediate region.
best_for: Those wanting French amenities in the Pacific

cities.description: Capital of the Solomon Islands on Guadalcanal — remote, undeveloped, and WWII-historic, best for the adventurous.
cities.visa_notes: No retirement visa; long stays via work or business permits, handled case-by-case. Infrastructure is limited.
cities.healthcare_notes: National Referral Hospital is under-resourced; nearly all serious cases evacuate to Australia.
best_for: Those wanting true off-grid Pacific life
avoid_if: Anyone needing dependable healthcare or safety

cities.description: PNG's capital — resource-rich and culturally fascinating, but with serious safety concerns that limit its retiree appeal.
cities.visa_notes: No retirement visa; long stays via work or business permits. Most foreigners live in secured compounds.
cities.healthcare_notes: Port Moresby General Hospital is strained; expats use private clinics and evacuate to Australia for serious care.
best_for: Those with strong local ties and security arrangements
avoid_if: Almost all independent retirees due to crime

cities.description: Capital of the US territory of Guam — American systems and the US dollar in a tropical Western Pacific setting.
cities.visa_notes: Guam is a US territory, so US immigration rules apply — straightforward for US citizens, who can relocate freely. Others need US visas.
cities.healthcare_notes: Guam Memorial and Guam Regional Medical City provide US-style care; some specialist cases travel to Hawaii or the mainland.
best_for: US citizens wanting tropical living under US law
Those valuing the US dollar and US systems
avoid_if: Those wanting a wide range of local specialists

cities.description: Main island of the US Commonwealth of the Northern Marianas — quieter and cheaper than Guam, with beaches and US ties.
cities.visa_notes: Part of the US Commonwealth (CNMI) — US immigration framework applies. Straightforward for US citizens; others need US visas.
cities.healthcare_notes: Commonwealth Healthcare Corporation hospital handles basics; serious cases travel to Guam, Hawaii, or the Philippines.
best_for: US citizens wanting a quiet, affordable tropical base
